His comments followed a new bout of public diplomacy by social media, but were dismissed by Iran’s foreign ministry, which said a final decision on an agreement had not been reached

items.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, Tehran denies "final conclusion"
items.standfirst: Iran said Friday it had not yet reached a final conclusion on a deal to end the war with the U.S. despite President Trump announcing an agreement would be signed soon.
items.content: Iran said Friday it had not yet reached a final conclusion on a deal to end the war with the U.S. despite President Trump announcing an agreement would be signed soon.

items.title: Trump claims deal to end Iran war near as Tehran says 'nothing' finalised
items.standfirst: The US president says a "great settlement" to end the conflict has been reached. Iran says reports of a deal are "speculative".
items.content: The US president says a "great settlement" to end the conflict has been reached. Iran says reports of a deal are "speculative".

items.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
items.standfirst: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.
items.content: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

items.title: Trump claims US and Iran on verge of signing peace agreement, but Tehran says no final decision made
items.standfirst: Iranian leadership has not confirmed claim, after the US president announced that planned strikes on Iran had been cancelled Donald Trump claimed on Thursday that Washington and Tehran were on the verge of signing a peace agreement, and announced that he was cancelling fresh missile strikes, after two days of escalating attacks on Iran that threatened to collapse the fragile ceasefire.His comments followed a new bout of public diplomacy by social media, but were dismissed by Iran’s foreign ministry, which said a final decision on an agreement had not been reached. Continue reading...
items.content: Iranian leadership has not confirmed claim, after the US president announced that planned strikes on Iran had been cancelled Donald Trump claimed on Thursday that Washington and Tehran were on the verge of signing a peace agreement, and announced that he was cancelling fresh missile strikes, after two days of escalating attacks on Iran that threatened to collapse the fragile ceasefire.His comments followed a new bout of public diplomacy by social media, but were dismissed by Iran’s foreign ministry, which said a final decision on an agreement had not been reached. items.title: What comes next if an Iran agreement is finalized?
items.standfirst: President Trump announced Thursday that the U.S.had reached a "settlement" with Iran that would begin talks to possibly end the war. Andrew Borene, a senior fellow at the National Security Institute and a former senior intelligence official, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.
items.content: President Trump announced Thursday that the U.S.had reached a "settlement" with Iran that would begin talks to possibly end the war. Andrew Borene, a senior fellow at the National Security Institute and a former senior intelligence official, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

items.title: Trump says US and Iran have reached a ‘great settlement’

items.content: US President Donald Trump claims Washington and Tehran have reached a ‘great settlement’ and are finalising documents

items.title: Trump says deal with Iran could be signed in days
items.standfirst: President Trump said an agreement to end the war could be signed with the Iranian regime within days, but that some thorny issues would remain for later talks. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.
items.content: President Trump said an agreement to end the war could be signed with the Iranian regime within days, but that some thorny issues would remain for later talks. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

items.title: Trump says Iran peace deal is near after threatening new strikes
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire. Hours before, he threatened to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire. Hours before, he threatened to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.

items.title: Iranian state media says reports of a deal are 'speculative and nothing has been finalized'
items.standfirst: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.
items.content: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.

clusters.title: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO
clusters.neutral_headline: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO
bullet_summary: 6 million shares at $135 each, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire
SpaceX has made history with the biggest-ever IPO, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire

items.title: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO
items.standfirst: SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham breaks down the situation and what to expect from SpaceX's first day of trading. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham breaks down the situation and what to expect from SpaceX's first day of trading. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Raises $2.2 Billion in Japan Slice of Record-Breaking IPO
items.standfirst: SpaceX raised $2.2 billion from Japanese investors, according to a regulatory filing on Friday, as part of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering.
items.content: SpaceX raised $2.2 billion from Japanese investors, according to a regulatory filing on Friday, as part of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering.

items.title: Musk's SpaceX set to make biggest-ever initial public offering on Wall Street
items.standfirst: Elon Musk's space transport company SpaceX is set to begin trading on the US market on Friday with an offering of more than 555 million shares priced at at $135 each. The move is expected to place SpaceX in the top 10 of Wall Street's biggest companies and make Musk the world's first trillionaire.
items.content: Elon Musk's space transport company SpaceX is set to begin trading on the US market on Friday with an offering of more than 555 million shares priced at at $135 each. The move is expected to place SpaceX in the top 10 of Wall Street's biggest companies and make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

items.title: SpaceX set to go public, could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
items.standfirst: SpaceX is set to go public on Friday in what's expected to be the largest IPO in history, a move that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire and create thousands of new millionaires inside the company. Jo Ling Kent reports.
items.content: SpaceX is set to go public on Friday in what's expected to be the largest IPO in history, a move that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire and create thousands of new millionaires inside the company. Jo Ling Kent reports.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75bn ahead of record stock market debut
items.standfirst: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.
items.content: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

Continue reading...

items.title: Thai princess dies at age 47 after 3 years in hospital
items.standfirst: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, a lawyer and the eldest of the king's seven children, has died at 47 after three years in a hospital, royal officials said. She was an advocate for women's rights.

items.title: Thailand’s Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies aged 47 after years in a coma
items.standfirst: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol’s health had worsened since she was hospitalised in December 2022 with heart problems that left her gravely illThe eldest child of Thailand’s King Maha Vajiralongkorn has died aged 47, the palace has said, after nearly four years in a coma.Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, known in Thailand as Princess Bha, had been in hospital since December 2022 when she became gravely ill after having heart problems while out training her dogs. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term for drone flights over Pyongyang
clusters.neutral_headline: South Korea’s ex-President Yoon gets 30 years over drone operation
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, France24, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term for drone flights over Pyongyang
items.standfirst: South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home.
items.content: South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home.(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)

items.title: South Korea sentences ex-president Yoon to 30 years over North Korea drone incident
items.standfirst: South Korea on Friday sentenced ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for sending military drones into North Korea in 2024 in a bid to "heighten inter-Korean military tensions and manufacture a national crisis" enabling him to declare martial law. Yeol was in February given a life imprisonment sentence – which he is appealing – for leading an insurrection with his martial law declaration.
items.content: South Korea on Friday sentenced ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for sending military drones into North Korea in 2024 in a bid to "heighten inter-Korean military tensions and manufacture a national crisis" enabling him to declare martial law. Yeol was in February given a life imprisonment sentence – which he is appealing – for leading an insurrection with his martial law declaration.

items.title: South Korea’s ex-President Yoon gets 30 years over drone operation

items.title: Trump’s hand-picked Kennedy Center board mounts last-ditch effort to keep his name
items.standfirst: Board seeks to stay judge’s ruling that found Trump’s name was illegally added to Washington performing arts venueDonald Trump’s hand-picked board at the Kennedy Center is mounting a last-minute effort to keep his name on the facade of the performing arts facility before a court-ordered deadline to remove it by Friday.The board voted on Thursday to seek a stay of US district judge Christopher Cooper’s 29 May ruling that said Trump’s name was illegally added to the Kennedy Center, according to a person familiar with the move who requested anonymity to discuss a private meeting. Continue reading...

Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified,” he wrote in a post on X.The Middle East is being pulled deeper into crisis & the consequences reach far beyond the region.” Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. military says it disabled oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
items.standfirst: The U.S. military said it disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker after it “violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to transport oil from Iran.” Three Indian seafarers who were initially reported missing after the strike were confirmed dead by India’s shipping minister.
items.content: The U.S. military said it disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker after it “violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to transport oil from Iran.” Three Indian seafarers who were initially reported missing after the strike were confirmed dead by India’s shipping minister.

clusters.title: 'Civilians will be put in harm's way' expert warns as first autonomous drone kills revealed
clusters.neutral_headline: Fully autonomous drones killed soldiers for the first time
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Reuters Top News

items.title: 'Civilians will be put in harm's way' expert warns as first autonomous drone kills revealed
items.standfirst: A senior figure in the Ukrainian defence industry said that a test took place two years ago involving fully autonomous drones set to destroy anything in a given area, with confirmed casualties, according to a report in New Scientist. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Mariarosaria Taddeo, Professor of Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies at the Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford, explains that these systems "are fully indiscriminate, whether the system sees a combatant, a child, an old person, it won't be able or won't be designed to discriminate, so civilians are going to be put in harm's way".
items.content: A senior figure in the Ukrainian defence industry said that a test took place two years ago involving fully autonomous drones set to destroy anything in a given area, with confirmed casualties, according to a report in New Scientist. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Mariarosaria Taddeo, Professor of Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies at the Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford, explains that these systems "are fully indiscriminate, whether the system sees a combatant, a child, an old person, it won't be able or won't be designed to discriminate, so civilians are going to be put in harm's way".

items.title: Fully autonomous drones killed soldiers for the first time
items.standfirst: A senior figure in Ukraine’s defence industry says a test two years ago involved fully autonomous drones programmed to destroy anything in a designated area, and that the test resulted in confirmed casualties. [newscientist.com]
items.content: A senior figure in Ukraine’s defence industry says a test two years ago involved fully autonomous drones programmed to destroy anything in a designated area, and that the test resulted in confirmed casualties. [newscientist.com]

clusters.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
clusters.neutral_headline: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
items.standfirst: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injured. Five of the dead were from mainland China and two from Hong Kong. Continue reading...
items.content: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injured. Five of the dead were from mainland China and two from Hong Kong. Continue reading...

items.title: Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing
items.standfirst: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.
items.content: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.

clusters.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
clusters.neutral_headline: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World

items.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

items.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona’s towering architectural masterpiece
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing to the highest spire of the world’s tallest church, when he holds Mass at the Sagrada Família in Barcelona.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing to the highest spire of the world’s tallest church, when he holds Mass at the Sagrada Família in Barcelona.

clusters.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
clusters.neutral_headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
items.standfirst: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.
items.content: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes on Afghanistan kill at least 13
items.standfirst: Pakistan launched new airstrikes in Afghanistan after months of fighting between the two neighbours that killed hundreds.
items.content: Pakistan launched new airstrikes in Afghanistan after months of fighting between the two neighbours that killed hundreds.
